Don't forget to smash the like button if you gained value out of today's video! Hope you found it helpful!

If you're open to feedback, I found the music very distracting - the volume was a factor, but mainly the beat itself wasn't particularly relaxing or easy to ignore. Something slower and quieter works better in my opinion. Thank you for the video regardless
Sounds like the best plan is to audit the courses you want to learn for free, then enroll for a month to knock out the certs?

All courses are well worth it I have taken many of their courses even things I never would have taken.

It depends. If you have plenty of time and determination to finish many courses, the plus is worth it. Although I am determined, but I don't have much time. I can only finish one course a month. What I did is to apply financial aid and I  am only paying for 5 bucks per course. Still you can never go wrong for investing in your learning.
my only main question is are the certificate's real? and would they truly hold up on a resume or in a interview trying to land a job?

Remember people, it doesn't matter where you learn what you learn. Results are the only meaningful truths. Will you be able to provide something to the company you wanna work for, by studying through Coursera ( or any platform for that matter )? If the answer is yes, and if Coursera is what would motivate you and help you to study, then that is your path to success. Always use what you feel motivated by ( but also be mindful of scams, obviously coursera is not a scam ).
